lines.text: Client's a federal contract and a county landlord. [pause] Cheaper than a real shop.
[chuckles] Smells like about forty years of hog auction in here.
Six of them, all the same. The flag rides flat inside. [pause] We pull the air out, bake the moisture off the fabric, then put nitrogen in so the dye doesn't rot on the way up.
You called this weld out full through, all the way. [pause] That much heat's going to cook the fitting and pull it out of true.
(then) Move it here. Come in under the frame rail. [quietly] Nobody ever sees it.
So we build fake ones and switch them, right. [pause] The flags never come down.
The play is we build six containment units to federal spec and deliver them on time. [flatly] It's a job, son.
[quietly] Man could about fit in there.
[curious] How would a person even ride in a box.
Most of it's already there. Air in, breath scrubbed back out. [pause] I kept ninety men breathing under the ice for two years.
Cutting table's in Bay Two. [pause] Let's get the first frame tacked before lunch.
